topic.what: Sensitivity-Uncertainty Alignment (SUA) framework for analyzing and mitigating LLM failures under adversarial and ambiguous inputs
topic.how: Uses single scalar quantity SUAθ(x)=Sθ(x;ε)−λHθ(Y|x) measuring excess of distributional sensitivity over predictive entropy with consistency regularization and entropy alignment training
topic.why_matters: Unifies adversarial robustness and ambiguity handling; provides theoretical bounds on worst-case perturbed risk and calibration error; architecture-agnostic diagnostic tool

characteristics.what_it_measures: Upper bound on risk under worst-case perturbation achievable by minimizing SUA
characteristics.vs_baseline: improvement over standard adversarial training without explicit calibration
characteristics.context: Minimize expected positive part of SUA to bound Rob_θ(x)

characteristics.what_it_measures: Lower bound on calibration error from persistent SUA violation
characteristics.vs_baseline: calibration error reduction via entropy alignment
characteristics.context: SUA violation implies miscalibration in terms of Cθ(x)

characteristics.what_it_measures: Formal guarantees on abstention rule risk
characteristics.context: Inference-time abstention rule with threshold τ

characteristics.what_it_measures: Alignment gap between perturbation sensitivity and predictive uncertainty
characteristics.context: Positive SUA indicates miscalibration; negative indicates overcaution

novelty: unified framework connecting adversarial sensitivity and ambiguity collapse
theoretical bounds linking SUA minimization to worst-case risk and calibration
architecture-agnostic definitions valid for any conditional distribution model
SUA-TR training combining consistency regularization with entropy alignment
open_problems: scalability to larger models and broader tasks
handling distribution shift and OOD inputs
operationalization of latent ambiguity A(x) estimation
